issues.summary: Silent proxy: days_since_last_donation used as value for days_since_last_communication in model feature extraction
issues.suggestion: Add days_since_last_communication as a field on the Donor dataclass and populate it from real data, or remove the feature column and document why the proxy is acceptable.
evidence: Donor dataclass has no days_since_last_communication field, so this proxy is unavoidable and silently degrades model accuracy
FEATURE_COLS includes days_since_last_communication as a distinct column but the Donor model cannot supply it

issues.summary: CORS wildcard allow_origins=['*'] on donor behavioral data API
issues.suggestion: Set allow_origins to an explicit list of trusted origins (e.g. dashboard hostname) in production. Remove the wildcard before deployment.
evidence: CORSMiddleware configured with allow_origins=['*'] and allow_credentials=True
Comment in server.py says 'Tighten per environment' but no environment-specific override mechanism is present
Fundraising platform handles donor behavioral data that should not be accessible cross-origin by default

issues.summary: Module-level _embedding_model singleton creates hidden state across test runs
issues.suggestion: Move the embedding model cache inside GrantMatcher as an instance attribute, or use a module-level cache with an explicit reset function for test isolation.
evidence: _embedding_model is a module-level global set to None at import time
_get_embedding_model() mutates this global on first call with no cleanup mechanism
Tests mocking sentence_transformers must manually reset this global to avoid state leakage between runs

issues.summary: catboost ImportError fallback log message omits install instructions
issues.suggestion: Change the warning to include the install command: 'catboost not installed; falling back to rules-based scoring. Install with: pip install open-paws-fundraising[catboost]'
evidence: logger.warning message reads only: 'catboost not installed; falling back to rules-based scoring'
No install command or documentation reference is included
pyproject.toml defines a [catboost] optional extra that would fix this, but operators are not told about it

dimension_notes.naming_quality: Excellent domain naming. ChurnPredictor, GrantMatcher, RevenueForecaster, DonorCohort, DonorImpactReport — all names are precise. FEATURE_COLS, CAT_COLS, NUM_COLS are clear. HIGH_RISK_THRESHOLD, MEDIUM_RISK_THRESHOLD are domain-legible.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Churn prediction logic is clearly structured with documented thresholds. Grant matching keyword/semantic blend is well-explained. Revenue forecasting fallback chain is clear. The proxy issue in _predict_model is the one clarity gap.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Python with comprehensive type hints. Optional[float] for churn outputs. from __future__ import annotations throughout. Dataclasses for domain models. Pydantic for API layer.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: ChurnPredictor.predict() → list[Donor] with populated fields is a clean contract. GrantMatcher.match() return schema is documented in the docstring. API endpoints are well-typed via Pydantic.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: Graceful fallbacks throughout: catboost → sklearn → rules-based. statsmodels → moving average. sentence-transformers → keyword-only. Consistent fallback-first pattern.
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: donors/, grants/, forecasting/, impact/, api/ modules each have clear single responsibilities. Segments.py and models.py are correctly separated. Good module depth.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: The _predict_model proxy issue (using one field as a stand-in for another) reads as an AI shortcut. Minor. Otherwise clean.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Fundraising intelligence platform: churn prediction + grant matching + revenue forecasting + impact reporting. These four concerns fit together coherently. The API layer binds them appropriately.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: src/ module structure is clean. donors/, grants/, forecasting/, impact/ are the right abstractions. Dashboard is separated. 17 files total — compact.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: _keyword_score() is a well-written function — keyword extraction, geo penalty, budget penalty are all cleanly separated. _rules_based_risk() follows the same pattern.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: impact/reporting.py integrates with churn prediction output (DonorImpactReport built after churn scoring). grants/matcher.py is standalone. The inter-module dependencies are explicit and directional.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: Global _embedding_model singleton in matcher.py creates hidden state. ChurnPredictor lazy-loads well. Minor issues.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: Consistent Python 3.11 conventions. from __future__ import annotations, dataclasses, enums — all standard. Ruff for linting. mypy configured.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: sklearn, pandas, numpy, statsmodels, fastapi, pydantic — all well-maintained. Optional catboost and sentence-transformers handled gracefully. Reasonable for a fundraising ML platform.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: Five test files, each testing a different module. Domain rules are encoded as test docstrings. Privacy invariants are tested as first-class domain constraints. test_churn_predictor covers rules-based path well. Model-based path has minimal coverage (no trained model in test suite). Effective coverage ~60-65% of critical paths.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: FastAPI endpoints are clean: /donors/at-risk, /donors/analyze, /grants/match, /grants/draft, /grants/pipeline, /forecast/revenue. The surface is coherent with the platform's purpose.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: API key auth scopes all requests to org_id — no cross-org data access. Clean auth middleware with Depends pattern. CORS wildcard is the gap.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: No incomplete migrations detected. Codebase is coherent and complete for its stated scope.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Clean directory structure. src/ → donors/, grants/, forecasting/, impact/, api/. Dashboard separate. Tests mirror src structure.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: The four modules (churn, grant matching, forecasting, impact) are coherent with the platform purpose. Privacy-by-design: pseudonymous IDs throughout, PII invariant tests, no PII in feature vectors.
dimension_notes.advocacy_language_quality: preferred_campaign_topic examples use 'farmed animal welfare'. CampaignOutcome.outcome_type includes 'animals_helped'. Impact statements are movement-appropriate.
dimension_notes.advocacy_security_posture: Pseudonymous IDs, no PII in feature vectors, org-scoped API keys. The CORS wildcard and lack of rate limiting on API endpoints are gaps. Donor behavioral data deserves tighter security posture.
dimension_notes.advocacy_terminology_consistency: farmed animal welfare, campaign, coalition partner, sanctuary — all used correctly. No industry synonyms introduced.
dimension_notes.advocacy_data_sovereignty: This is the strongest dimension: privacy-by-design throughout. Pseudonymous donor_id. FEATURE_COLS explicitly excludes PII. Privacy invariant tests verify no PII slips into feature vectors. Coalition partner controls their data.
dimension_notes.advocacy_ux_inclusivity: API and dashboard are for org staff, not public users. Streamlit dashboard is reasonably accessible. Graceful fallbacks (rules-based when no model) improve resilience for smaller orgs.
dimension_notes.advocacy_tool_integration: Integrates with CRM data (donor behavioral exports), grant databases (seed_grants.json), and FastAPI makes it composable. Dashboard for human review. Moderate integration with broader OP ecosystem.

dimension_judgment.advocacy_data_sovereignty: Highest score in this repo. Privacy-by-design is deeply implemented: pseudonymous IDs, PII-free feature vectors, privacy invariant tests. This is genuinely excellent for a donor-data platform.
dimension_judgment.test_strategy: Tests are honest and domain-grounded. The model-based prediction path is untested (no trained model in test suite), but the rules-based fallback path is well-covered. Score reflects actual coverage.
